channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-22
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` and `giladfeldman/escicheck` projects have made strides in updating their documentation and syncing changes with other tools, such as effectcheck. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has focused on localization and deployment fixes, including enabling RLS on the schema_migrations bookkeeping table. Additionally, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has prioritized security hardening and bug fixes, including improvements to the 'qwen' feature and logging. These connections between projects demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and continuous improvement, and we celebrate the progress made towards more robust and reliable research tools.

115 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

docpluck34 commits
regcheck32 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck15 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 4 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a fix was implemented to "strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose" in the render function, as stated in the commit message. The documentation was also updated in this branch, including a handoff document for the RC-T implementation, which logs the deferral and close of collabra.77859 adjudication. Additionally, the documentation was updated to include the specification for the RC-T table-region prose-contamination issue, which confirmed a 2-layer root cause and outlined a fix plan. The branch also saw updates to the documentation for triage and learnings, specifically for the v2.4.95 corpus assessment, as well as changes to enforce library and app version-pin sync through directives and QA/review/deploy gates.

4 commit(s) today:

  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the 'staging' branch, where security features were hardened. The 'qwen' feature was re-added, and subsequent commits focused on improving and firming up its formatting. Additionally, the logging was improved, and efforts were made to "hunt down" an OSF bug, which suggests that bug fixes were a focus. A push for redeploy was also made, indicating that changes were prepared for deployment. Furthermore, the repository was updated to address issues, with a specific commit mentioning "push for redeploy", implying that the changes were being prepared for a new deployment.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from escicheckapp@bd6f007, which corresponds to effectcheck version 0.6.5, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the main branch, synced changes from escicheckapp@dec37b1, which is associated with effectcheck version 0.6.4. These commits brought the main branch up to date with the specified versions of effectcheck. The commits were made in close succession, with both occurring on the same day, June 21, 2026.

1 commit(s) today:

  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1f1a89a [main] sync from escicheckapp@e060e98 (effectcheck v0.6.2) (#16) — Gilad Feldman
  • f7e25f6 [sync/20260525-150441-b801333] sync from escicheckapp@b801333 (effectcheck v0.6.2) — github-actions[bot]
  • d0db89d [main] sync from escicheckapp@5093197 (effectcheck v0.6.1) (#14) — Gilad Feldman
  • a44f236 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1ebe05f (effectcheck v0.5.18) (#13) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. The commit message does not specify any additional details about the link or its purpose. There are no other branches or commits to report on in this update.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
